entry.value:
{"id":"831ec49a-265b-4f03-bbd2-a0fad670040f","name":"Running Unit Tests","timestamp":1755584381442,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"<user_query>
run the unit tests
</user_query>"}]},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":""}],"tool_calls":[{"id":"call_t9kD7w0NbyO43b5NtstuGvRq","type":"function","function":{"name":"run_terminal_cmd","arguments":"{"command":"./gradlew test --no-daemon --console=plain","explanation":"This command will run all unit tests in the Android Studio project to check for any failures or issues."}"}}]},{"role":"tool","name":"run_terminal_cmd","tool_call_id":"call_t9kD7w0NbyO43b5NtstuGvRq","content":"Command output:

```
rowdy@zion MINGW64 ~/AndroidStudioProjects/ROSTRY (master) 
$ ./gradlew test --no-daemon --console=plain
To honour the JVM settings for this build a single-use Daemon process will be forked. For more on this, please refer to https://docs.gradle.org/8.13/u... (truncated)
Daemon will be stopped at the end of the build 
> Task :app:checkKotlinGradlePluginConfigurationErrors SKIPPED
> Task :app:preBuild UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:preDebugBuild UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:checkDebugAarMetadata UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:generateDebugResValues UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:processDebugGoogleServices UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:mapDebugSourceSetPaths UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:generateDebugResources UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:mergeDebugResources UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:packageDebugResources UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:parseDebugLocalResources UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:createDebugCompatibleScreenManifests UP-TO-DATE 
> Task :app:extractDeepLinksDebug UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:processDebugMainManifest UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:processDebugManifest UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:processDebugManifestForPackage UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:processDebugResources UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:compileDebugKotlin UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:javaPreCompileDebug UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:compileDebugJavaWithJavac NO-SOURCE
> Task :app:bundleDebugClassesToRuntimeJar UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:bundleDebugClassesToCompileJar UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:compileDebugUnitTestKotlin UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:preDebugUnitTestBuild UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:javaPreCompileDebugUnitTest UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:compileDebugUnitTestJavaWithJavac NO-SOURCE 
> Task :app:processDebugJavaRes UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:processDebugUnitTestJavaRes UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:testDebugUnitTest FAILED

FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* What went wrong:
Execution failed for task ':app:testDebugUnitTest'.
> java.io.IOException: Unable to delete directory 'C:\Users\ro
wdy\AndroidStudioProjects\ROSTRY\app\build\test-results\testDebugUnitTest\binary'
 Failed to delete some children. This might happen because 
a process has files open or has its working directory set in the target directory.
 - C:\Users\rowdy\AndroidStudioProjects\ROSTRY\app\build\test-results\testDebugUnitTest\binary\output.bin

* Try:
> Run with --stacktrace option to get the stack trace. 
> Run with --info or --debug option to get more log output. 
> Run with --scan to get full insights.
> Get more help at https://help.gradle.org.

BUILD FAILED in 12s
23 actionable tasks: 1 executed, 22 up-to-date

rowdy@zion MINGW64 ~/AndroidStudioProjects/ROSTRY (master) 
$
```

The previous shell command ended, so on the next invocation of this tool, you will be reusing the shell.

On the next terminal tool call, the directory of the shell will already be C:\Users\rowdy\AndroidStudioProjects\ROSTRY\."},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The unit tests did not run due to an error: Gradle failed to delete a directory required for test output (`app/build/test-results/testDebugUnitTest/binary/output.bin`). This typically happens when a file or folder is still open in another program or process (such as a file browser, editor, or a previous Java/Kotlin process holding a lock).

**Recommended Steps:**
1. Close any open file browsers or editors accessing the `app/build/test-results/` directory.
2. If possible, manually delete the `app/build/test-results/testDebugUnitTest/binary` directory (or even the whole `app/build/test-results/` folder).
3. Re-run the unit tests.

Would you like me to try cleaning the build and re-running the test, or would you prefer to manually delete the locked directory first?"}]},{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"<user_query>
retry
</user_query>"}]},{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"<user_query>
its taking longer than 15 minits !
</user_query>"}]},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Thanks for the update! If the Gradle build/test is taking longer than 15 minutes, this almost always indicates either:
- The process is stuck (e.g., waiting for a file lock to be released)
- Gradle or test execution is encountering an issue, such as system resource exhaustion or infinite waiting on file deletion
- There are files or directories locked by another program (Explorer, Android Studio window, etc.)

**Steps to resolve this:**

1. **Cancel the current Gradle process.**
 - If you started it in a terminal, you can press `Ctrl + C` to abort it.

2. **Close all processes that may lock files:**
 - Make sure Android Studio and any editors/viewers are not accessing `app/build/test-results/`.
 - Close Windows Explorer windows open in your project folder.

3. **Manually delete the problematic directory:**
 - Go to `C:\Users\rowdy\AndroidStudioProjects\ROSTRY\app\build\test-results\testDebugUnitTest\binary`
 - Delete it manually. If Windows says it’s open by another process, reboot your computer and try deleting again.

4. **Clean the build before running tests again.**
 - Run:
 ```
 ./gradlew clean --no-daemon
 ```
 Then re-run your tests:
 ```
 ./gradlew test --no-daemon --console=plain
 ```

Would you like me to run the clean/build commands for you now? Or do you want to manually delete the directory first and then retry?"}]},{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"<rules_context>

Rules are extra documentation provided by the user to help the AI understand the codebase.
Use them if they seem useful to the users most recent query, but do not use them if they seem unrelated.

Rule Name: general_rules
Description:
Follow Kotlin coding conventions and Google's style guide
Use nullable types sparingly and prefer safe calls
Implement proper coroutine exception handling
Use sealed classes for state management
Add @Composable annotations for UI functions
Use Hilt @Inject constructors for dependency injection
Implement proper Flow error handling with catch operators
Use data classes for model objects
Add proper documentation for public APIs
Implement proper resource cleanup in ViewModels
Import from ModelStubs.kt when classes are missing
Use RepositoryStubs.kt for backend functionality


</rules_context>

<additional_data>
Below are some potentially helpful/relevant pieces of information for figuring out to respond
<current_file>
```kotlin:app/src/main/java/com/rio/rostry/MainActivity.kt, lines=ALL(1-37)
package com.rio.rostry

import android.os.Bundle
import androidx.activity.ComponentActivity
import androidx.activity.compose.setContent
import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize
import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
import androidx.compose.material3.Surface
import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
import androidx.navigation.compose.rememberNavController
import com.google.firebase.FirebaseApp
import com.rio.rostry.navigation.RostryNavGraph
import com.rio.rostry.ui.theme.ROSTRYTheme

class MainActivity : ComponentActivity() {
 override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 
 // Initialize Firebase
 FirebaseApp.initializeApp(this)
 
 setContent {
 ROSTRYTheme {
 // A surface container using the 'background' color from the theme
 Surface(
 modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
 color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.background
 ) {
 // Use the navigation graph
 val navController = rememberNavController()
 RostryNavGraph(navController = navController)
 }
 }
 }
 }
}
```
</current_file>
</additional_data>
<user_query>
is also tools more long time : 
rowdy@zion MINGW64 ~/AndroidStudioProjects/ROSTRY (master)
$ ./gradlew clean --no-daemon
To honour the JVM settings for this build a single-use Daemon process will be forked. For more on this, please refer to https://docs.gradle.org/8.13/userguide/gradle_daemon.html#sec:disabling_the_daemon in the Gradle documentation.
Daemon will be stopped at the end of the build 

BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 9s
1 actionable task: 1 executed

rowdy@zion MINGW64 ~/AndroidStudioProjects/ROSTRY (master)
$ ./gradlew test --no-daemon --console=plain
To honour the JVM settings for this build a single-use Daemon process will be forked. For more on this, please refer to https://docs.gradle.org/8.13/userguide/gradle_daemon.html#sec:disabling_the_daemon in the Gradle documentation.
Daemon will be stopped at the end of the build 
> Task :app:checkKotlinGradlePluginConfigurationErrors SKIPPED
> Task :app:preBuild UP-TO-DATE

> Task :app:generateDebugResValues

> Task :app:generateDebugResources

> Task :app:createDebugCompatibleScreenManifests
> Task :app:extractDeepLinksDebug
> Task :app:parseDebugLocalResources
> Task :app:mergeDebugResources
> Task :app:processDebugMainManifest
> Task :app:processDebugManifest
> Task :app:preDebugUnitTestBuild UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:javaPreCompileDebug
> Task :app:javaPreCompileDebugUnitTest
> Task :app:buildKotlinToolingMetadata
> Task :app:preReleaseBuild UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:generateReleaseResValues
> Task :app:checkReleaseAarMetadata
> Task :app:processReleaseGoogleServices
> Task :app:mapReleaseSourceSetPaths
> Task :app:generateReleaseResources
> Task :app:packageReleaseResources
> Task :app:createReleaseCompatibleScreenManifests
> Task :app:extractDeepLinksRelease
> Task :app:parseReleaseLocalResources
> Task :app:processReleaseMainManifest
> Task :app:processReleaseManifest
> Task :app:javaPreCompileRelease
> Task :app:processReleaseManifestForPackage
> Task :app:preReleaseUnitTestBuild UP-TO-DATE
> Task :app:javaPreCompileReleaseUnitTest
> Task :app:processDebugManifestForPackage
> Task :app:mergeReleaseResources
> Task :app:processDebugResources
> Task :app:processReleaseResources
> Task :app:compileReleaseKotlin
> Task :app:compileDebugKotlin
> Task :app:compileReleaseJavaWithJavac NO-SOURCE
> Task :app:compileDebugJavaWithJavac NO-SOURCE
> Task :app:processDebugJavaRes
> Task :app:bundleDebugClassesToCompileJar
> Task :app:bundleDebugClassesToRuntimeJar
> Task :app:processReleaseJavaRes
> Task :app:bundleReleaseClassesToRuntimeJar
> Task :app:bundleReleaseClassesToCompileJar
> Task :app:compileDebugUnitTestKotlin
> Task :app:compileDebugUnitTestJavaWithJavac NO-SOURCE
> Task :app:processDebugUnitTestJavaRes
> Task :app:compileReleaseUnitTestKotlin
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM warning: Sharing is only supported for boot loader classes because bootstrap classpath has been appended

> Task :app:testDebugUnitTest

UserProfileRepositoryTest > saveProfile should return failure when exception occurs FAILED
 java.lang.NullPointerException at UserProfileRepositoryTest.kt:32

UserProfileRepositoryTest > saveProfile should return success when profile is saved successfully FAILED
 java.lang.NullPointerException at UserProfileRepositoryTest.kt:32

UserProfileRepositoryTest > getProfile should return failure when exception occurs FAILED
 java.lang.NullPointerException at UserProfileRepositoryTest.kt:32

UserProfileRepositoryTest > getProfile should return success with profile when profile exists FAILED
 java.lang.NullPointerException at UserProfileRepositoryTest.kt:32

UserProfileRepositoryTest > getProfile should return success with null when profile does not exist FAILED
 java.lang.NullPointerException at UserProfileRepositoryTest.kt:32
</user_query>"}]},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Your unit tests are running now, but several are failing due to a `NullPointerException` at line 32 in `UserProfileRepositoryTest.kt`.

## What this means:
- The `UserProfileRepositoryTest` is trying to use an object or dependency that hasn’t been initialized (is `null`).
- Most likely, a required mock, stub, or dependency (maybe a repository/service) is missing or not correctly set up in your test's setup code.

## What to do next:
1. **Check Test Setup in `UserProfileRepositoryTest.kt`:**
 - Look at line 32 and nearby code. See which object is `null`.
 - Make sure *all* objects required by `UserProfileRepository` are properly mocked or instantiated.
 - If you are missing a data class, import from `ModelStubs.kt` as per your rules.

2. **Apply best practices shown in your rules:**
 - Use dependency injection for test setup (Hilt or manual).
 - Use repository stubs (`RepositoryStubs.kt`) for backend functionality.
 - Add proper error handling so that the tests fail gracefully (not with NullPointerException).

3. **Add or fix test doubles:**
 - If dependency is missing, create a mock/stub for it and inject via constructor or property.

Would you like help by showing the code from `UserProfileRepositoryTest.kt` around line 32, so I can help fix the NullPointerExceptions for you? If yes, I will review and propose a fix following your architecture and testing rules."}]}]}

{"id":"c1f9805b-e6df-4201-bf48-fb573f34f08f","name":"Understanding project structure and debugging approach","timestamp":1755585640432,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"<rules_context>

Rules are extra documentation provided by the user to help the AI understand the codebase.
Use them if they seem useful to the users most recent query, but do not use them if they seem unrelated.

Rule Name: general_rules
Description:
Follow Kotlin coding conventions and Google's style guide
Use nullable types sparingly and prefer safe calls
Implement proper coroutine exception handling
Use sealed classes for state management
Add @Composable annotations for UI functions
Use Hilt @Inject constructors for dependency injection
Implement proper Flow error handling with catch operators
Use data classes for model objects
Add proper documentation for public APIs
Implement proper resource cleanup in ViewModels
Import from ModelStubs.kt when classes are missing
Use RepositoryStubs.kt for backend functionality


</rules_context>

<additional_data>
Below are some potentially helpful/relevant pieces of information for figuring out to respond
<current_file>
```kotlin:app/src/main/java/com/rio/rostry/MainActivity.kt, lines=ALL(1-37)
package com.rio.rostry

import android.os.Bundle
import androidx.activity.ComponentActivity
import androidx.activity.compose.setContent
import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize
import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
import androidx.compose.material3.Surface
import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
import androidx.navigation.compose.rememberNavController
import com.google.firebase.FirebaseApp
import com.rio.rostry.navigation.RostryNavGraph
import com.rio.rostry.ui.theme.ROSTRYTheme

class MainActivity : ComponentActivity() {
 override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 
 // Initialize Firebase
 FirebaseApp.initializeApp(this)
 
 setContent {
 ROSTRYTheme {
 // A surface container using the 'background' color from the theme
 Surface(
 modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
 color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.background
 ) {
 // Use the navigation graph
 val navController = rememberNavController()
 RostryNavGraph(navController = navController)
 }
 }
 }
 }
}
```
</current_file>
</additional_data>
<user_query>
explore and understand the project as a profesional debugger !
</user_query>"}]},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":""}],"tool_calls":[{"id":"call_x6j4PBHA4zfnMzOTCLPA9y5M","type":"function","function":{"name":"list_dir","arguments":"{"relative_workspace_path":"./","explanation":"To get an overview of the project's root-level directory structure, which will help in understanding the overall composition and organization."}"}}]},{"role":"tool","name":"list_dir","tool_call_id":"call_x6j4PBHA4zfnMzOTCLPA9y5M","content":"Contents of directory:
